source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane, Other Lifting - Fire

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, West Delta Block 152

actions_taken: Personnel donned life jackets and proceeded to muster area
Life capsule engine started in preparation for launch
Adjacent platform (WD 133-F) alerted to shut in
Glycol system isolated from production system
Damaged components shipped onshore for fit-for-service inspection and NDE
Asbestos panels removed and replaced by third-party contractors
event.summary: On January 17, 2019, at 0930 hours, a fire occurred in the glycol dehydration unit on Renaissance Offshore's WD 152-A platform. During platform startup following an extended shutin, the Field Foreman started the glycol reboiler and increased temperature from 200°F to 250°F without proper circulation time or adherence to industry guidelines. The #1 compressor shut down due to high pressure, and the Field Foreman left the glycol unit unattended. Rapid temperature rise caused overpressure in the reboiler due to high water content (21% vs. typical 5-10%), forcing Triethylene Glycol (TEG) and liquid contaminates out of the still column onto the hot stack (operating temperature >660°F). The TEG ignited upon contact with the stack, creating a large flame. Personnel responded by activating ESD, fire alarms, and manually fighting the fire with fire reels. No injuries or pollution were reported. Damage included fire-damaged aluminum shell, broken sight glass, melted pressure gauges, burned insulation, heat-damaged motor/belt, damaged electrical wiring, and damaged asbestos panels. Root causes were inadequate startup procedures and lack of operator training on glycol phase vaporization characteristics.
recommendations: Insulate the glycol stack to eliminate hot surfaces as ignition sources
Enhance training for personnel on chemical and effluent characteristics during liquid-to-gas phase vaporization and handling of contaminated or saturated glycol
Improve glycol startup and shutdown procedures with specific operational definitions (temperature set points vs. vague instructions like 'bringing up slowly')
Ensure operators are trained and familiar with specific glycol reboiler startup and shutdown procedures
Utilize third-party expertise to conduct onsite operator competency assessments
Conduct Hazard Analysis on all glycol reboilers at Renaissance facilities to ensure safe working condition

Create Safety Alert or Safety Bulletin addressing advanced training on glycol phase vaporization, requirement for manned glycol startup operations, specific startup procedures with circulation time requirements, and consideration of reboiler stack insulation

threats.name: Rapid temperature increase during glycol reboiler startup without proper circulation
High water content in glycol causing phase vaporization and overpressure
Release of TEG and hydrocarbon condensate from reboiler still column
Contact of flammable glycol/condensate with hot stack surface (>660°F)

consequences.name: Structural damage to compressor building and glycol unit

controls.name: Glycol startup procedure with temperature rate specifications
controls.barrier_role: Prevent rapid temperature increase and overpressure
controls.lod_basis: Industry standard (Gly-Tech TEG Systems Manual recommends heating to no more than 220°F before startup circulation)

supporting_text: The Renaissance Glycol start up procedure did not provide specifics on the rate of temperature increase when starting the reboiler after an extended shut in.
According to Gly-Tech's TEG Systems Manual (an industry recognized training manual), 'heat the reboiler to no more than 220 degrees F before start up circulation.'
The Operator increased the temperature from 200 to 250 degrees F without circulating glycol and allowing enough time to boil off water.

controls.name: Operator training on glycol phase vaporization and contaminated glycol handling
controls.barrier_role: Ensure operators understand risks and proper procedures for glycol startup
controls.lod_basis: Industry best practice and regulatory requirement

supporting_text: Renaissance failed to provide training which would have allowed Operators to understand characteristics of phase vaporization and handling contaminated or saturated glycol.
Renaissance identified deficiencies in training and supervision.

controls.name: Continuous operator attendance during glycol startup
controls.barrier_role: Enable early detection and intervention if startup parameters deviate
controls.lod_basis: Gly-tech company recommendation for continuous attendance during glycol startups

supporting_text: Glycol operations experts from the company Gly-tech recommend that operators continuously attend Glycol start-ups. However, Renaissance Operators left the glycol skid unattended during start-up.
The Field Foreman left the glycol unit unattended while the temperature was increasing from 200 degrees F to 250 degrees F.
BSEE recognizes the failure to attend the start up as a contributing cause of the incident.

controls.name: Glycol system maintenance to control water content
controls.barrier_role: Prevent high water content that exacerbates overpressure during startup
controls.lod_basis: Typical glycol water content should be 5-10%; incident had 21%

supporting_text: The high water content of 21% exacerbated the overpressure. The typical water content of the reboiler is 5-10%.
Renaissance did not maintain the glycol system properly such that the water content of the glycol reached 21%.

controls.name: Reboiler stack insulation to prevent ignition source
controls.barrier_role: Prevent contact of released glycol/condensate with hot surfaces
controls.lod_basis: Stack temperature exceeds TEG ignition temperature (660°F); TSE melts at 1170°F indicating operating temperature >660°F

supporting_text: Lack of insulation on the Reboiler Stack allowed an ignition source as TEG exited the still.

The TSE on the stack melts at 1170 degrees F which implies that the operating temperature may reach temperatures greater than 660 degrees F. The ignition temperature of Triethylene Glycol (TEG) is 660 degrees F.
Renaissance will insulate the glycol stack as part of the corrective action.

controls.name: Pressure Safety Valve (PSV) on glycol reboiler
controls.barrier_role: Relieve overpressure in reboiler to prevent rupture
controls.lod_basis: Standard pressure relief device on pressurized vessels

supporting_text: Water and other contaminates changed phase so rapidly that the pressure build up within the vessel opened the Pressure Safety Valve (PSV) and forced TEG out of the still.

controls.name: Emergency Shutdown (ESD) system activation
controls.barrier_role: Isolate equipment and stop operations in emergency
controls.lod_basis: Standard emergency response system on offshore platforms

supporting_text: At approximately 0930 hours, the Mechanic witnessed a large flame emanating from the north side of the platform. He alerted the Operators of a fire and pulled the Emergency Shutdown (ESD).

controls.name: Fire alarm activation and personnel response
controls.barrier_role: Alert personnel and initiate emergency response procedures
controls.lod_basis: Standard emergency alarm and response system

supporting_text: The Person In Charge (PIC) activated the fire alarm and manned the #4 fire reel on the west side of the upper deck. The Operator manned the # 9 fire reel on the east side of the lower deck. The Field Forman manned the # 8 fire reel located on the east side of the lower deck.

controls.name: Manual fire suppression with fire reels
controls.lod_basis: Standard fire suppression equipment on offshore platforms

supporting_text: After personnel extinguished the fire, they assessed the damage and reported the fire to the Renaissance Production Superintendent.

controls.name: Personnel muster and evacuation procedures
controls.barrier_role: Protect personnel from fire hazard and enable evacuation if necessary

supporting_text: The Mechanic and the Night Operator donned their life jackets and went to the primary muster area located on the south side of the lower deck. The Mechanic started the life capsule engine, preparing to launch.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
